postings.title: Frontend Developer
postings.description:
Brightline Commerce builds checkout and storefront experiences for mid-market retailers, and we're looking for a Frontend Developer to join our product engineering team. You'll ship customer-facing features weekly, collaborate closely with designers working in Figma, and help us keep our component library healthy as it scales across four product lines.

Day to day, you'll build responsive interfaces in React and TypeScript, wire them up to our REST APIs, and manage client state with Redux. We care a lot about performance and accessibility, so a11y-minded engineering isn't optional here — it's part of how we define done.

Requirements:
- 3+ years building production web apps with JavaScript and React
- Strong TypeScript skills and comfort with modern CSS (Flexbox, Grid)
- Experience consuming REST APIs and handling loading/error states gracefully
- Working knowledge of Git-based workflows and code review
- Solid grasp of responsive design and cross-browser quirks

Nice to have:
- Experience with Next.js and server-side rendering
- Familiarity with Tailwind CSS or Sass for styling at scale
- Testing experience with Jest and React Testing Library
- Exposure to GraphQL or Apollo Client

We offer a hybrid schedule (three days in our downtown Austin office), competitive pay, equity, and a real commitment to sane on-call. If you like owning features end to end and sweating the details that make a UI feel fast, we'd love to talk.

postings.title: Junior Frontend Developer
postings.description:
Maple & Finch is a small design-led product studio that builds marketing sites and web apps for early-stage startups. We're hiring a Junior Frontend Developer who's early in their career but hungry to grow. You'll pair regularly with senior engineers, translate Figma designs into pixel-accurate interfaces, and learn how a real production codebase fits together.

Most of our work is built with React and TypeScript, styled with Tailwind CSS, and bundled with Vite. You don't need to know all of it on day one — we care more about fundamentals and curiosity than a long resume.

Requirements:
- A solid handle on HTML, CSS, and JavaScript fundamentals
- Some exposure to React (bootcamp projects, internships, or personal work all count)
- Comfort with Git basics: branches, commits, pull requests
- An eye for detail and responsive design
- Willingness to ask questions and take feedback

Nice to have:
- Any TypeScript experience
- Familiarity with Tailwind CSS or Sass
- Have played with Vite, Next.js, or similar tooling
- Basic understanding of accessibility (a11y) principles

This is a fully remote role open to candidates in US time zones. We offer mentorship, a learning stipend, and a genuinely supportive team that reviews code kindly. If you've built a few things you're proud of and want to level up fast alongside people who care about craft, apply — a portfolio or GitHub link matters more to us than a perfect CV.

postings.title: Senior Frontend Developer
postings.description:
Northwind Analytics builds a real-time dashboarding platform used by data teams at hundreds of companies. We're looking for a Senior Frontend Developer to lead architecture on our visualization-heavy web app, mentor mid-level engineers, and set the technical direction for how we build UI at scale.

Our stack is React and TypeScript on top of Next.js, with a GraphQL data layer and heavy use of Redux for complex client state. You'll own performance work on data-dense views, drive our accessibility roadmap, and shape the testing culture across the frontend org.

Requirements:
- 6+ years of frontend experience, with deep React and TypeScript expertise
- Proven track record architecting large single-page applications
- Strong experience with GraphQL and normalizing complex API data
- Hands-on with Next.js and modern build tooling (Vite or Webpack)
- A rigorous approach to testing with Jest and React Testing Library
- Fluency in responsive design and accessibility (a11y) standards

Nice to have:
- Experience with data visualization libraries (D3, Visx, or similar)
- Cypress or Playwright for end-to-end coverage
- Familiarity with Docker and CI/CD pipelines
- Prior experience mentoring or leading a small team

This is a fully remote role with flexible hours; we have teammates across eight time zones and asynchronous communication is core to how we work. We offer strong compensation, meaningful equity, and the autonomy to make real architectural decisions. If you want ownership rather than tickets handed down, this is the role.

postings.title: Frontend Developer
postings.description:
Cobalt Health Systems builds patient-facing portals and clinician tools for hospital networks. Because our software is used in real care settings, accessibility and reliability aren't features — they're requirements. We're hiring a Frontend Developer to join our web team and help us build interfaces that are usable by everyone.

You'll work in React with TypeScript, collaborate with a design team that lives in Figma, and integrate with a mix of REST APIs and GraphQL endpoints. Expect to spend real time on keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and WCAG conformance.

Requirements:
- 3-5 years building web applications with React and JavaScript
- Strong CSS skills, including Sass and responsive design
- Demonstrated experience with accessibility (a11y) and WCAG guidelines
- Comfort consuming REST APIs; willingness to learn our GraphQL layer
- Familiarity with Git and collaborative code review

Nice to have:
- TypeScript in a production codebase
- Testing with Jest and React Testing Library
- Experience in healthcare, fintech, or another regulated domain
- Exposure to Webpack configuration and build optimization

This is an on-site role at our Boston office (near South Station), with a strong team culture and a mission that actually matters. We offer excellent benefits, including full health coverage and a 401(k) match. If you take pride in building software that works for people with disabilities and holds up under real clinical use, we'd like to meet you.

postings.title: Frontend Developer
postings.description:
Loomstack builds developer tooling for teams shipping infrastructure-as-code. Our web console is where users manage deployments, and we want a Frontend Developer to make it faster, clearer, and more delightful. You'll join a product team of six and work closely with backend engineers and a product designer.

We build with React, TypeScript, and Next.js, style with Tailwind CSS, and talk to our services over GraphQL. The console handles a lot of real-time state, so thoughtful state management with Redux matters here.

Requirements:
- 3+ years of frontend development with React and TypeScript
- Solid experience with GraphQL and reactive UI patterns
- Comfort with Tailwind CSS and modern responsive design
- Familiarity with Next.js and the React Server Component model
- Git-based workflow and a habit of writing reviewable PRs

Nice to have:
- Redux or another predictable state container at scale
- Testing with Jest, React Testing Library, and Cypress
- Interest in DevOps tooling, Docker, or Kubernetes concepts
- Experience with Vite or other modern build tooling

This is a hybrid role based in Berlin — two days a week in our Kreuzberg office. We offer a relocation package for EU-based candidates, 28 days of vacation, and a learning budget. Working language is English. If you enjoy building tools that other engineers rely on every day and care about a snappy, well-tested UI, we'd love to hear from you.

postings.title: Frontend Developer (Contract)
postings.description:
Pier 9 Digital is an agency that builds marketing sites and web apps for consumer brands. We're looking for a contract Frontend Developer for a six-month engagement (with likely extension) to help us deliver several client projects that are picking up steam.

You'll jump between codebases, so adaptability matters. Most projects use React, TypeScript, and Next.js, with styling split between Tailwind CSS and Sass depending on the client. You'll translate Figma designs into production interfaces and make sure everything is responsive and accessible.

Requirements:
- 4+ years of professional frontend experience
- Strong React, JavaScript, and TypeScript skills
- Fluent in HTML and CSS, including Sass and Tailwind CSS
- Comfortable turning Figma files into pixel-accurate, responsive UI
- Self-directed; able to onboard to a new codebase quickly
- Solid Git workflow

Nice to have:
- Next.js and static site generation experience
- Familiarity with headless CMS platforms (Contentful, Sanity)
- Testing with Jest or Cypress
- Basic REST API integration and accessibility (a11y) knowledge

This is a 1099 contract role, fully remote within the US, roughly 40 hours a week. Rate is competitive and depends on experience. You'll work with a friendly, low-drama team of designers and developers who ship real work for recognizable brands. If you like variety, clear scopes, and finishing things, this contract is a good fit. Please include a portfolio when you apply.

postings.title: Senior Frontend Developer
postings.description:
Vantage Payments builds the merchant dashboard that thousands of businesses use to manage transactions, disputes, and payouts. We're hiring a Senior Frontend Developer to lead frontend work on our core product surface and raise the bar for quality across the team.

Our stack is React, TypeScript, and Redux, bundled with Webpack, backed by REST APIs and a growing GraphQL gateway. As a senior engineer you'll own complex features end to end, drive our testing strategy, and mentor two mid-level developers.

Requirements:
- 6+ years of frontend engineering, mostly with React
- Expert-level TypeScript and strong JavaScript fundamentals
- Deep experience with Redux and managing complex application state
- Comfort integrating REST APIs and working with GraphQL
- A mature testing practice using Jest and React Testing Library
- Strong grasp of responsive design and accessibility (a11y)

Nice to have:
- Cypress for end-to-end testing
- Webpack configuration and bundle-size optimization
- Experience in fintech or another regulated, high-trust domain
- Familiarity with feature flagging and progressive rollouts

This is a hybrid role — three days a week in our Manhattan office near Union Square. Compensation is strong and includes equity, and we take work-life balance seriously. Security and correctness are part of our culture because we handle money. If you want senior-level ownership on a product where quality genuinely matters, apply and let's talk.

postings.title: Frontend Developer
postings.description:
Solstice Learning builds an online education platform used by universities and bootcamps to deliver interactive courses. We're looking for a Frontend Developer to help us build engaging, accessible learning experiences that work for students on any device.

Our frontend is built with React, TypeScript, and Next.js, styled with Tailwind CSS, and tested with Jest and React Testing Library. We use Vite for our internal tooling and talk to a REST API for course data. Accessibility is central to our mission — our students include learners with a wide range of needs.

Requirements:
- 3+ years building production React applications
- Solid TypeScript and modern JavaScript
- Strong HTML and CSS, with Tailwind CSS experience
- Real commitment to accessibility (a11y) and responsive design
- Experience writing tests with Jest and React Testing Library
- Comfortable with Git and pull-request workflows

Nice to have:
- Next.js and server-side rendering
- Experience with Vite or Webpack build tooling
- Familiarity with video or interactive content players
- Exposure to GraphQL

This role is fully remote for candidates in US or Canadian time zones. We offer flexible hours, a generous PTO policy, and a team that genuinely cares about the impact of our product. Twice a year we gather in person for a week of collaboration. If you want your frontend work to help people learn — and you care about building interfaces everyone can use — we'd love to hear from you.

postings.title: Frontend Developer
postings.description:
Harbor Robotics builds fleet-management software for warehouse automation. Our web console lets operators monitor and control robots in real time, and we need a Frontend Developer who can build responsive, reliable interfaces for a demanding operational environment.

You'll work in React and TypeScript, manage real-time state with Redux, and render live telemetry streamed from our backend over GraphQL subscriptions and REST APIs. Our UI runs on large control-room displays as well as tablets on the warehouse floor, so responsive design and performance are both critical.

Requirements:
- 3-5 years of frontend experience with React and JavaScript
- Strong TypeScript skills
- Experience with Redux or similar state management for real-time data
- Comfort integrating REST APIs and, ideally, GraphQL
- Solid CSS and responsive design across screen sizes
- Git-based collaboration

Nice to have:
- Experience with WebSockets or real-time data streams
- Testing with Jest, React Testing Library, or Cypress
- Familiarity with Vite or Webpack tooling
- Interest in robotics, hardware, or industrial software

This is an on-site role at our San Diego facility, where being near the robots and the operators genuinely helps you build better software. We offer competitive pay, equity, full benefits, and the rare chance to see your UI directly control physical machines. If you like systems that bridge software and the real world — and you sweat responsiveness and reliability — come build with us.

postings.title: Backend Developer (Node.js)
postings.description:
Brightloom Labs builds analytics tooling for logistics companies, and we're growing our backend team. You'll own services that ingest millions of shipment events a day, expose clean REST APIs to our frontend, and keep latency predictable under load. This is a hands-on role where you'll design schemas, write tests, and ship to production most weeks. We work in small squads, review each other's PRs, and care about readable code over cleverness.

What you'll do day to day: build and maintain Node.js services in TypeScript, model data in PostgreSQL, cache hot paths in Redis, and wire everything into our Docker-based CI/CD pipeline. You'll also help debug production incidents and improve our observability.

Requirements:
- 3+ years building backend services with Node.js and TypeScript
- Strong SQL skills, ideally with PostgreSQL
- Comfortable designing and consuming REST APIs
- Experience writing unit tests (we use Jest) and working in a CI/CD workflow
- Familiarity with Docker and containerized local development

Nice to have:
- Exposure to Redis for caching or rate limiting
- Experience with Express or NestJS
- Any hands-on time with AWS (we run on ECS)
- Comfort reading basic SQL query plans

We offer competitive pay, a hybrid schedule (three days in our Austin office), solid health coverage, and a real learning budget. If you like owning things end to end and working with people who explain their reasoning, we'd love to talk.

postings.title: Senior Backend Engineer
postings.description:
Northgate Systems powers billing infrastructure for B2B SaaS companies. We're hiring a senior backend engineer to lead design on our payments and ledger services, where correctness matters more than anything. You'll set technical direction for a small team, mentor mid-level engineers, and be the person who thinks hard about edge cases before they become incidents.

Our stack is Go and Python, with PostgreSQL as our source of truth and Kafka moving events between microservices. We deploy to Kubernetes on AWS and lean heavily on automated testing to sleep at night.

Requirements:
- 6+ years of backend experience, with real depth in Go or Python
- Deep understanding of PostgreSQL, including transactions and isolation levels
- Experience designing microservices that communicate over Kafka or a similar broker
- Comfort operating services in Kubernetes and AWS
- A track record of writing thorough unit and integration tests
- Ability to lead design discussions and mentor others

Bonus points:
- Experience with gRPC for internal service communication
- Familiarity with Redis and idempotency patterns
- Prior work on financial, billing, or ledger systems
- Contributions to CI/CD tooling or developer platform work

This is a fully remote role open to US-based candidates. We offer strong equity, four weeks of PTO, and a genuinely calm on-call rotation. If you want ownership of systems where getting the details right actually matters, reach out.

postings.title: Junior Backend Developer
postings.description:
Pinewood Digital is a product studio building web apps for healthcare and education clients. We're looking for a junior backend developer to join our team and grow with us. You don't need years of experience, but you should be genuinely curious, willing to ask questions, and excited to write code that real users depend on. You'll be paired with a senior mentor from day one.

You'll start by fixing bugs and adding endpoints to our existing services, then take on larger features as you ramp up. Expect lots of code review, pairing sessions, and honest feedback.

Requirements:
- Some experience with a backend language, ideally Node.js or Python (bootcamp, internship, or personal projects all count)
- Basic understanding of REST APIs and how the web works
- Familiarity with at least one database, such as MySQL or MongoDB
- Willingness to learn TypeScript and write unit tests
- Clear written communication and a collaborative attitude

Nice to have:
- Any exposure to Express or a similar web framework
- Basic comfort with Git and command-line tools
- Curiosity about Docker or how apps get deployed
- A GitHub profile or portfolio we can look at

We offer a supportive environment, a defined learning path, mentorship, and a hybrid schedule in our Denver office. This is a great first backend job for someone who wants to build strong fundamentals with people who are patient teachers.

postings.title: Backend Engineer, Platform
postings.description:
Halcyon Cloud provides a managed data platform for mid-market companies across Europe. Our platform team builds the internal APIs and services that every product team depends on, so reliability and clean interfaces are the whole job. We're a remote-first company with teammates across several time zones, and we work asynchronously with a few overlap hours.

You'll build and maintain microservices in Java, expose both REST and GraphQL APIs, and use RabbitMQ to decouple workloads. Data lives in PostgreSQL and MongoDB depending on the service, and everything ships through Docker and Kubernetes.

Requirements:
- 4+ years building backend services, with solid Java experience
- Practical knowledge of designing REST APIs; some GraphQL exposure
- Experience with a message broker such as RabbitMQ or Kafka
- Comfort with PostgreSQL and at least one NoSQL store like MongoDB
- Hands-on experience with Docker and Kubernetes
- Discipline around unit testing and CI/CD

Nice to have:
- Familiarity with Go for smaller services
- Experience running workloads on AWS
- Knowledge of Redis for caching or session storage
- Comfort working fully asynchronously

We hire across the EU and UK on local contracts, offer generous vacation, a home-office budget, and twice-yearly team gatherings. If you like being the dependable foundation other teams build on, this role is for you.

postings.title: Backend Developer
postings.description:
Ferro & Vine is a fast-growing e-commerce brand, and our engineering team keeps the storefront, inventory, and order systems humming. We're hiring a backend developer to work on the services behind checkout, catalog, and fulfillment. You'll join a team of eight engineers who ship daily and take pride in a tidy codebase.

Our backend is built in Python with a growing set of Node.js services. We rely on PostgreSQL for transactional data, Redis for caching and queues, and REST APIs to tie the frontend and mobile apps together. We run on AWS and deploy through a Docker-based CI/CD pipeline.

Requirements:
- 3-5 years of backend development experience
- Strong Python skills; comfort picking up Node.js and TypeScript
- Solid grasp of relational databases, especially PostgreSQL
- Experience building and maintaining REST APIs
- Familiarity with Redis and background job processing
- Habit of writing unit tests and participating in code review

Bonus:
- Experience with AWS services (we use RDS, S3, and SQS)
- Exposure to Docker and infrastructure-as-code
- Prior work on e-commerce or high-traffic consumer products
- Interest in performance profiling and query optimization

We offer a hybrid setup with two days a week in our Chicago Loop office, employee product discounts, strong health benefits, and a team that genuinely enjoys working together. Come help us scale a business people love.

postings.title: Senior Backend Developer (Go)
postings.description:
Meridian Freight Tech builds the routing and dispatch backbone for regional trucking fleets. Our systems make real-time decisions on thousands of shipments, so we care deeply about throughput, correctness, and clean service boundaries. We're looking for a senior backend developer to help us scale our microservices and mentor a growing team.

We write our core services in Go, with some Python for data tooling. Services talk to each other over gRPC, events flow through Kafka, and state lives in PostgreSQL and Redis. Everything runs in Kubernetes on AWS with a mature CI/CD setup.

Requirements:
- 6+ years of backend experience, several of them in Go
- Strong understanding of microservice design and inter-service communication
- Hands-on experience with gRPC and event streaming via Kafka
- Deep PostgreSQL knowledge and comfort with Redis
- Production experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and AWS
- Commitment to testing and reliable CI/CD practices

Nice to have:
- Experience with real-time or geospatial systems
- Familiarity with observability tooling (Prometheus, Grafana)
- Interest in performance tuning and profiling Go services
- Prior technical leadership or mentoring experience

This role is remote across the US and Canada. We offer meaningful equity, a four-day on-call rotation shared across the team, a hardware budget, and yearly in-person offsites. If moving physical goods with well-built software sounds satisfying, let's talk.

postings.title: Backend Developer (Node.js / NestJS)
postings.description:
Tanglewood Media runs a subscription platform for independent creators, and our backend team keeps content delivery, payments, and creator tools running smoothly. We're hiring a backend developer to build new features across our API and help modernize older services. We're headquartered in Berlin but work remote-first with core hours in Central European Time.

Our services are built with Node.js and NestJS in TypeScript. We use PostgreSQL as our primary database, MongoDB for flexible content metadata, and Redis for caching. Our GraphQL API serves web and mobile clients, and we deploy via Docker.

Requirements:
- 3+ years of backend development with Node.js and TypeScript
- Experience with NestJS or a comparable structured framework like Express
- Strong PostgreSQL skills and comfort with schema design
- Experience building GraphQL or REST APIs
- Familiarity with Redis and caching strategies
- Solid unit testing habits (Jest) and CI/CD experience

Nice to have:
- Experience with MongoDB for semi-structured data
- Exposure to AWS or another cloud provider
- Familiarity with Docker and container orchestration
- Interest in payments, subscriptions, or creator economy products

We offer flexible hours, 28 days of vacation, a remote-work stipend, and quarterly meetups in Berlin. You'll work with a small, kind team that ships thoughtfully and supports creators doing interesting work. If that resonates, we'd love to hear from you.

postings.title: Backend Engineer
postings.description:
Cobalt Health Data builds secure data infrastructure for hospital networks. Because we handle sensitive records, we hold a high bar for correctness, testing, and clear service boundaries. We're hiring an experienced backend engineer to design and build the APIs that power our clinical data pipelines.

Our stack is primarily Java and Python. We expose REST APIs internally, move data between microservices with RabbitMQ, and store records in PostgreSQL and MySQL depending on the domain. We containerize with Docker, orchestrate with Kubernetes, and run on AWS with strict CI/CD gates.

Requirements:
- 5+ years of backend engineering experience
- Strong skills in Java or Python (ideally both)
- Solid experience designing REST APIs and microservices
- Deep relational database knowledge across PostgreSQL and MySQL
- Experience with a message broker like RabbitMQ
- Rigorous approach to unit and integration testing
- Comfort with Docker, Kubernetes, and a cloud provider such as AWS

Bonus:
- Experience in healthcare, HIPAA, or another regulated domain
- Familiarity with data privacy and encryption practices
- Exposure to Kafka for higher-volume event streams
- Interest in building internal developer tooling

This is an onsite-leaning hybrid role in Boston, three days a week in office. We offer strong compensation, excellent health benefits, a 401(k) match, and work that genuinely improves patient care. If building careful, well-tested systems in a domain that matters appeals to you, apply.

postings.title: Backend Developer (Python)
postings.description:
Larkspur Analytics helps retailers make sense of their sales data. Our backend team builds the ingestion pipelines and APIs that turn raw transaction feeds into dashboards our customers rely on every morning. We're hiring a backend developer who enjoys working close to data and wants to see their work drive real business decisions.

We write most of our services in Python, with a few Go services for performance-sensitive paths. Data lands in PostgreSQL, we cache aggregates in Redis, and events move through Kafka. We expose REST APIs to the frontend, containerize with Docker, and deploy on AWS.

Requirements:
- 3-5 years of backend development, primarily in Python
- Strong SQL and hands-on PostgreSQL experience
- Experience building REST APIs consumed by web clients
- Familiarity with an event streaming system like Kafka
- Comfort with Redis for caching
- Solid unit testing discipline and CI/CD familiarity

Nice to have:
- Any Go experience for high-throughput services
- Exposure to data pipeline or ETL work
- Familiarity with Docker and AWS (we use ECS and RDS)
- Interest in query optimization and data modeling

We offer a hybrid schedule in our Seattle office, competitive pay, strong benefits, and a team that values clarity and steady shipping over crunch. If you like turning messy data into something useful and well-structured, we'd be glad to meet you.

postings.title: Full-Stack Developer (React / Node)
postings.description:
Brightloom Technologies is a Series B startup building workflow automation software for logistics teams. We're a small, fast-moving engineering group looking for a Full-Stack Developer who's comfortable owning features end to end, from the database schema to the pixels users click.

Day to day, you'll ship features across our web app and backend services, participate in code reviews, and pair with designers to turn Figma mockups into production UI. We run two-week sprints and practice agile/scrum, so you should be comfortable estimating work and demoing at the end of each cycle.

Requirements:
- 3+ years of professional experience building web applications
- Strong JavaScript and TypeScript skills
- Hands-on experience with React and Node.js (Express or similar)
- Comfortable designing and querying PostgreSQL databases
- Experience building and consuming REST APIs
- Solid Git workflow habits and familiarity with CI/CD pipelines

Nice to have:
- Experience with Next.js for server-rendered React
- Exposure to Prisma or another modern ORM
- Familiarity with Docker and containerized local development
- Testing experience with Jest
- AWS deployment experience

We offer competitive salary, equity, health coverage, and a genuine say in the technical direction of the product. Our stack is modern and our tech debt is low, because we care about writing code we can maintain. If you like small teams where your work is visible and your opinions matter, we'd love to talk. This role is hybrid, with two days a week in our downtown Austin office and flexibility around the rest.

postings.title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer
postings.description:
Meridian Health Systems builds patient-engagement tools used by clinics across the country. We're hiring a Senior Full-Stack Engineer to lead development on our provider-facing scheduling platform. This is a high-ownership role: you'll set technical direction, mentor mid-level engineers, and make architecture decisions that affect the whole team.

You'll work across a TypeScript codebase spanning a Next.js frontend and Node.js services, backed by PostgreSQL. Because we operate in healthcare, reliability and correctness matter deeply, so a strong testing culture is non-negotiable here.

Requirements:
- 6+ years building production web applications
- Deep expertise in TypeScript, React, and Node.js
- Strong experience with Next.js and PostgreSQL
- Proven track record designing REST APIs and, ideally, GraphQL schemas
- Experience with Docker and container orchestration
- Comfortable owning CI/CD pipelines and deployment on AWS
- Testing discipline with Jest and end-to-end tools like Playwright

Nice to have:
- Experience with Prisma for data access
- Background working in a HIPAA or otherwise regulated environment
- Kubernetes experience for production workloads
- Familiarity with observability tooling

We're fully remote within the US, with quarterly team gatherings. Compensation is competitive and includes equity, a generous learning budget, and strong health benefits (as you'd expect from a health company). We practice agile/scrum with a pragmatic bent, favoring shipping over ceremony. If you want to write software that measurably improves how people access care, and you're the kind of engineer others learn from, we'd like to hear from you. Bring examples of systems you've designed and scaled.

postings.title: Junior Full-Stack Developer
postings.description:
Pinegrove Labs is a digital product studio that builds web apps for clients ranging from early-stage startups to established brands. We're looking for a Junior Full-Stack Developer to join our team and grow alongside experienced engineers who genuinely enjoy mentoring.

This is an entry-level role, so we care more about your fundamentals and your willingness to learn than about a long resume. You'll start by fixing bugs and building small features, then take on larger pieces of work as you find your footing. Expect thorough code reviews, plenty of pairing, and a supportive environment where questions are welcome.

Requirements:
- Working knowledge of JavaScript and HTML/CSS
- Some experience with React, whether from projects, bootcamp, or internships
- Basic understanding of how REST APIs work
- Familiarity with Git and version control workflows
- Eagerness to learn and take feedback well

Nice to have:
- Exposure to TypeScript
- Any experience with Node.js or Express
- Familiarity with a database like PostgreSQL or MongoDB
- Tailwind CSS experience
- Coursework or side projects you can show us

We offer a structured onboarding program, a dedicated mentor for your first six months, and a clear path toward a mid-level role. Our office in Denver's RiNo district is dog-friendly and stocked with good coffee; we work in-office three days a week. Benefits include health insurance, a transit stipend, and paid time to work on learning goals. If you've built a few things you're proud of and want to turn coding into a career, send us your GitHub or a portfolio. We review every application.

postings.title: Full-Stack Developer (GraphQL / TypeScript)
postings.description:
Cobalt & Finch is a fintech company building spend-management tools for small businesses across the EU. We're expanding our engineering team and looking for a Full-Stack Developer who enjoys working close to the data and cares about clean, well-typed code.

Our platform is a TypeScript monorepo: a React frontend talking to Node.js services through a GraphQL API, with PostgreSQL underneath managed via Prisma. You'll build features across the stack, contribute to our shared component library, and help keep our schema clean as the product grows.

Requirements:
- 3-5 years of full-stack development experience
- Strong TypeScript across frontend and backend
- Solid React skills and comfort with modern hooks-based patterns
- Experience building GraphQL APIs (Apollo or similar)
- Node.js backend experience
- PostgreSQL and hands-on ORM experience, ideally Prisma
- Comfortable with Git-based workflows and CI/CD

Nice to have:
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- Docker for local development and deployments
- AWS experience
- Testing with Jest and Playwright
- Prior fintech or payments experience

We're remote-first across European time zones, with a hub office in Amsterdam for anyone who wants it. We work in short, focused sprints and keep meetings light. Compensation is benchmarked to Western European rates regardless of where you live, and includes equity, a home-office budget, and 28 days of holiday. We value engineers who ask good questions and push back when something doesn't make sense. If building financial software that people actually trust sounds like your kind of problem, apply with a short note about something you've shipped recently.

postings.title: Full-Stack Software Engineer
postings.description:
Northwind Analytics helps mid-market retailers make sense of their sales data. We're a profitable, bootstrapped company with a stable product and a growing customer base, and we're adding a Full-Stack Software Engineer to help us build the next generation of our dashboard platform.

You'll work on everything from data-heavy React interfaces to the Node.js and Express services that power them. Our data lives in both PostgreSQL and MongoDB depending on the workload, so comfort moving between relational and document stores is a real plus. We value engineers who think about performance, because our customers load a lot of data at once.

Requirements:
- 4+ years building full-stack web applications
- Strong JavaScript and TypeScript
- Proficiency with React and building responsive UIs
- Node.js and Express backend experience
- Working knowledge of PostgreSQL and at least one NoSQL store like MongoDB
- Experience designing REST APIs
- Git and CI/CD familiarity

Nice to have:
- Docker and container-based deployment experience
- AWS infrastructure knowledge
- Data visualization library experience
- Jest testing experience
- Interest in query optimization and caching

We're based in Chicago's Loop and work a hybrid schedule, three days in-office. Because we're bootstrapped, we don't chase hype; we build things that last and we don't do death marches. Benefits include full health coverage, a 401(k) match, profit sharing, and genuinely reasonable hours. If you're tired of rewrites and want to work somewhere the software actually ships and stays shipped, we'd love to meet you. Tell us about a performance problem you're proud of having solved.

postings.title: Full-Stack Developer
postings.description:
Lumen Grid builds monitoring software for renewable energy installations, from rooftop solar to utility-scale wind. We're a climate-tech company with real customers and real revenue, hiring a Full-Stack Developer to help us turn sensor data into interfaces operators can actually use.

The role spans our React and Tailwind CSS frontend and the Node.js services behind it. You'll build dashboards that visualize live telemetry, wire up the APIs that feed them, and help keep our deployment pipeline healthy. We care about writing tested, containerized code that we can deploy with confidence.

Requirements:
- 3+ years of full-stack experience
- Strong JavaScript and TypeScript
- Solid React experience; comfort with Tailwind CSS for styling
- Node.js backend development
- Experience with REST APIs and PostgreSQL
- Docker for local and production environments
- Familiarity with CI/CD and Git workflows

Nice to have:
- AWS deployment experience
- Time-series or streaming data experience
- Testing with Jest and Playwright
- Next.js familiarity
- Interest in the energy or climate space

We're fully remote across US time zones and operate on an agile cadence without excessive process. Compensation includes equity in a company we believe is genuinely making a difference, plus solid health benefits, a home-office stipend, and flexible hours. Our team is small enough that your work has clear impact and you'll never wonder whether what you built shipped. If you want your day job to line up with your values, and you like building interfaces on top of messy real-world data, we'd love to talk. Send us a note and, if you have it, something you've built that you're proud of.

postings.title: Senior Full-Stack Developer
postings.description:
Kestrel Interactive is a studio building collaborative tools for creative teams. We're looking for a Senior Full-Stack Developer to anchor our platform team and raise the bar on how we design, test, and ship software.

Our product is a real-time collaborative web app: a React and Next.js frontend, Node.js services, PostgreSQL with Prisma, and a GraphQL layer that ties it together. As a senior engineer, you'll own significant slices of the architecture, review others' work thoughtfully, and help define the standards the rest of the team follows.

Requirements:
- 6+ years of full-stack development
- Expert-level TypeScript across the stack
- Deep React and Next.js experience
- Strong Node.js backend skills
- Production experience with PostgreSQL and Prisma
- GraphQL API design experience
- Strong testing habits with Jest and Playwright
- Comfort with Docker, CI/CD, and AWS

Nice to have:
- Experience with real-time systems (WebSockets, CRDTs)
- Tailwind CSS
- Mentorship or tech-lead experience
- Interest in developer experience and tooling

We're based in downtown Toronto and work a hybrid schedule, with two anchor days in-office and the rest flexible. Compensation is competitive for the Canadian market and includes equity, extended health and dental, an RRSP match, and a real professional-development budget. We practice agile/scrum but keep the ceremony light, trusting engineers to manage their own time. If you're a senior developer who wants scope, autonomy, and teammates who care about craft, come build with us. In your application, tell us about a system you designed and what you'd change about it now.

postings.title: Full-Stack Engineer (Node / React)
postings.description:
Harbor Point Software builds B2B tools for the maritime shipping industry, digitizing workflows that still run on spreadsheets and email. We're hiring a Full-Stack Engineer to help modernize an industry that's overdue for it.

You'll work across a full stack of React on the frontend and Node.js with Express on the backend, storing data in MongoDB. Our customers operate around the clock, so reliability matters, and we lean on automated testing and solid CI/CD to keep things stable. This is a hands-on building role with a lot of surface area.

Requirements:
- 3-6 years of full-stack web development
- Proficient in JavaScript; TypeScript experience preferred
- Strong React skills
- Node.js and Express backend experience
- Experience with MongoDB or another document database
- REST API design experience
- Comfortable with Git and CI/CD pipelines

Nice to have:
- Docker for containerized development
- AWS experience
- Jest and Playwright testing
- Tailwind CSS
- Domain experience in logistics or supply chain

We're located in Seattle's Pioneer Square and work hybrid, three days on-site. Benefits include full medical, dental, and vision, a 401(k) match, commuter benefits, and a genuinely calm engineering culture; we plan carefully so we don't have to scramble. Our codebase is well-tested and our on-call is quiet, and we intend to keep it that way. If you like the idea of bringing modern software to an industry that runs the global economy, and you want to work somewhere that respects your time, we'd like to hear from you. Applications with a short intro get read first.

postings.title: Full-Stack Developer (Contract-to-Hire)
postings.description:
Vellum Studio is a boutique agency that builds custom web applications for nonprofits and mission-driven organizations. We're looking for an early-career Full-Stack Developer for a contract-to-hire role, with the expectation that a strong contributor converts to full-time within a few months.

Because we work with a variety of clients, you'll touch a range of projects, most built with React on the frontend and Node.js on the backend. It's a great role for someone who wants breadth and exposure to real client work early in their career. You'll have a senior developer reviewing your code and available for questions throughout.

Requirements:
- 1-2 years of experience, or a strong portfolio of projects
- Comfortable with JavaScript and React
- Basic Node.js and REST API experience
- Familiarity with a database, either PostgreSQL or MongoDB
- Git fundamentals
- Good written communication for a remote team

Nice to have:
- TypeScript exposure
- Tailwind CSS
- Any experience with Next.js
- Jest or other testing experience
- Prior agency or freelance work

This role is fully remote within the US. The contract period is paid at a competitive hourly rate, and converting to full-time brings health benefits, paid time off, and a stake in the projects we take on. We're deliberate about the clients we work with, and the work tends to feel meaningful because of it. If you're building your career and want variety, mentorship, and projects you can feel good about, apply with a link to your work and a couple sentences about what kind of problems you enjoy.

postings.title: Data Scientist
postings.description:
Northwind Analytics helps mid-market retailers turn transaction data into pricing and inventory decisions. We're hiring a Data Scientist to join a five-person team that owns models end to end, from framing the problem with stakeholders to shipping predictions into production.

Day to day you'll build demand-forecasting and churn models, run experiments, and translate messy results into recommendations our merchandising clients actually use. You'll partner closely with data engineers, so you won't be stuck maintaining pipelines, but you should be comfortable writing clean, testable code.

Requirements:
- 3+ years building predictive models in a commercial setting
- Strong Python and SQL; fluent with pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn
- Solid grounding in statistics and machine learning fundamentals (regression, tree-based methods, cross-validation)
- Experience designing and analyzing A/B testing
- Comfortable working in Jupyter and communicating findings through clear data visualization

Nice to have:
- Exposure to Spark for larger-than-memory datasets
- Tableau for stakeholder-facing dashboards
- Familiarity with feature engineering for time-series problems
- Experience with any major cloud ML platform

We offer a hybrid schedule (three days in our downtown Austin office), a genuine learning budget, and the kind of small-team ownership that gets rare as companies scale. If you like seeing your work change what a business does next week rather than next year, we'd like to talk. Salary range is $115,000-$140,000 depending on experience, plus equity.

postings.title: Senior Data Scientist, Growth
postings.description:
Larkspur Financial is a consumer lending startup building fairer credit products. Our Growth team is looking for a Senior Data Scientist to lead measurement and modeling for acquisition and retention. This is a high-autonomy role reporting to the Head of Data, and you'll set the technical direction for how we run experiments across the funnel.

You'll design our experimentation framework, build propensity and lifetime-value models, and mentor two junior scientists. Expect to spend real time with Marketing and Product leaders, so you need to be as comfortable in a strategy conversation as in a notebook.

Requirements:
- 6+ years in applied data science, with at least two owning experimentation at scale
- Expert Python and SQL; deep facility with pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn
- Rigorous statistics background: causal inference, power analysis, and A/B testing beyond the basics
- Track record shipping machine learning models that moved a business metric
- Strong feature engineering instincts and clear data visualization skills (Matplotlib or similar)

Bonus points:
- Spark and distributed data processing
- AWS SageMaker for model training and deployment
- Experience in fintech, lending, or another regulated domain
- R for statistical prototyping

We're fully remote within the US with quarterly onsites in Denver. Compensation is $175,000-$210,000 plus meaningful early-stage equity, and we cover 100% of medical premiums. We care more about how you reason through ambiguity than which frameworks are on your resume.

postings.title: Junior Data Scientist
postings.description:
Meridian Health Data partners with hospital systems to reduce readmissions and improve care coordination. We're adding a Junior Data Scientist to our clinical analytics group. This is a mentored role designed for someone early in their career who wants to grow into full model ownership within a supportive team.

You'll work alongside senior scientists and clinicians to clean and explore patient datasets, prototype predictive models, and build visualizations that help care teams act. We pair every project, review each other's code, and expect questions rather than guesswork.

Requirements:
- Degree in a quantitative field, or equivalent bootcamp plus project experience
- Working knowledge of Python and SQL
- Familiarity with pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn from coursework or internships
- Comfortable in Jupyter and eager to learn statistics and machine learning on the job
- Careful, detail-oriented approach to messy real-world data

Nice to have:
- Any exposure to data visualization tools (Matplotlib, Tableau)
- Coursework in deep learning or an intro TensorFlow project
- Interest in healthcare or biology
- A public GitHub or portfolio we can look at

Because of the data we handle, this role is onsite in our Boston office at least four days a week, and a background check is required. Starting salary is $85,000-$100,000 with full benefits and a professional development stipend. We hire for curiosity and follow-through, and we're happy to teach the rest.

postings.title: Data Scientist, Experimentation Platform
postings.description:
Cobalt Streaming is a music and podcast platform with 20 million monthly listeners. Our Experimentation Platform team builds the tools and analyses that let every product squad ship with confidence. We're hiring a Data Scientist who lives and breathes A/B testing and wants to shape how a whole company makes decisions.

You'll own the statistical engine behind our experimentation system, consult on tricky test designs, and build the metrics and dashboards teams rely on. Half your impact is technical and half is education, so you should enjoy explaining variance reduction to a skeptical PM.

Requirements:
- 4+ years in data science or quantitative analysis
- Strong Python and SQL; comfortable with pandas and NumPy
- Deep applied statistics: hypothesis testing, sequential testing, and the failure modes of A/B testing
- Experience turning analysis into clear data visualization for non-technical audiences
- Ability to work independently across time zones

Nice to have:
- Spark for processing event-level data at scale
- scikit-learn for variance-reduction and heterogeneous-effect models
- Tableau or a similar BI tool
- Prior work on an internal platform or developer-facing product

This is a remote role open across the US, with optional gatherings in Seattle twice a year. We offer $130,000-$160,000, equity, unlimited PTO that people actually take, and a hardware budget. If you think most dashboards lie and want to build ones that don't, apply.

postings.title: Machine Learning Data Scientist
postings.description:
Vireo Robotics builds autonomous material-handling robots for warehouses. Our perception and planning teams generate enormous amounts of sensor data, and we need a Data Scientist who can turn it into models that make our robots smarter and safer.

You'll work on problems spanning classical machine learning and deep learning: anomaly detection on telemetry, predictive maintenance, and computer-vision model evaluation. You'll collaborate with robotics engineers and have access to a real fleet, so your models get tested in the physical world quickly.

Requirements:
- 4+ years applying machine learning to real problems
- Strong Python; fluent with NumPy, pandas, and scikit-learn
- Hands-on deep learning experience with PyTorch or TensorFlow
- Solid statistics and feature engineering fundamentals
- Comfortable prototyping in Jupyter and presenting results with clear data visualization

Bonus:
- Experience with sensor, time-series, or robotics data
- Spark for large-scale data processing
- AWS SageMaker or another managed training platform
- SQL for pulling structured fleet data

This role is onsite in San Jose four days a week because you'll want to be near the hardware. We offer $145,000-$180,000, strong equity, and the rare chance to watch your model change how a physical machine behaves the same afternoon you trained it. We value engineers who are curious about the whole system, not just the model.

postings.title: Data Scientist
postings.description:
Tavora Commerce powers checkout and fraud tooling for thousands of online stores across North America. We're looking for a Data Scientist to join our Risk team and build models that catch fraud without punishing good customers.

You'll develop and monitor classification models, dig into false-positive complaints, and partner with our engineering team to deploy scoring into the live payment flow. Fraud is adversarial and fast-moving, so you'll iterate constantly and measure everything.

Requirements:
- 3+ years building supervised machine learning models in production
- Strong Python and SQL; daily use of pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn
- Solid grasp of statistics, class imbalance, and thoughtful feature engineering
- Experience with A/B testing or champion/challenger model rollouts
- Clear communicator who can defend a model to Risk and Compliance stakeholders

Nice to have:
- Spark for feature computation over large transaction volumes
- Deep learning experience with PyTorch for sequence models
- Tableau for monitoring dashboards
- Exposure to payments, fintech, or fraud domains

This is a hybrid role based in our Toronto office (two days in-office). Compensation is CAD $120,000-$150,000 plus benefits and an RRSP match. Work authorization in Canada is required; we're not able to sponsor for this position. If you like problems where the adversary adapts to your last move, you'll fit right in.

postings.title: Senior Data Scientist
postings.description:
Helvetia Mobility runs a shared e-bike and scooter network across a dozen European cities. We're hiring a Senior Data Scientist to lead demand forecasting and fleet-rebalancing models that keep vehicles where riders need them.

You'll own forecasting and optimization workstreams end to end, from spatial-temporal modeling to the metrics that tell operations teams where to move vehicles overnight. You'll also mentor two mid-level scientists and help set our modeling standards as the team grows.

Requirements:
- 6+ years in applied data science with production ownership
- Expert Python and SQL; deep experience with pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn
- Strong statistics and forecasting background; comfortable with spatial and time-series feature engineering
- Experience running A/B testing on operational changes
- Fluent English and clear data visualization skills for pan-European stakeholders

Bonus:
- Spark for city-scale trip data
- Deep learning with TensorFlow or PyTorch for demand models
- AWS SageMaker for training and deployment
- Tableau or Matplotlib for operations dashboards

This is a hybrid role in Berlin (three days in-office). We offer a competitive salary of EUR 85,000-105,000, 30 days of holiday, and relocation support for candidates already eligible to work in the EU. Our data is genuinely spatial and genuinely messy, and the models we build change what happens on real streets every night. If that appeals, get in touch.

postings.title: Data Scientist, Marketing Analytics
postings.description:
Brightseed Media is a digital publisher with a portfolio of lifestyle and news brands. Our Marketing Analytics team is hiring a Data Scientist to measure campaign effectiveness and model audience behavior across our properties.

You'll build attribution and segmentation models, quantify the incremental impact of campaigns, and give our growth marketers a clear read on what's working. This role blends statistics, storytelling, and a healthy skepticism of vanity metrics.

Requirements:
- 3+ years in analytics or data science, ideally in media, marketing, or ecommerce
- Strong SQL and Python; comfortable with pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn
- Solid statistics foundation and hands-on A/B testing experience
- Skilled at data visualization and building dashboards in Tableau
- Able to present to marketing leaders without drowning them in jargon

Nice to have:
- R for statistical modeling and reporting
- Feature engineering for user-behavior and clickstream data
- Spark for processing large event logs
- Familiarity with media-mix or attribution modeling

This is a hybrid position based in Chicago, with two flexible in-office days per week. We offer $110,000-$135,000, a strong benefits package, and a culture that treats analytics as a partner to the business rather than a reporting function. If you get energized by finding the real story hidden in noisy marketing data, we'd love to hear from you.

postings.title: Data Scientist (Deep Learning)
postings.description:
Auralux AI builds voice and audio intelligence tools for contact centers. We're hiring a Data Scientist with strong deep learning chops to improve our speech models and the analytics layer that sits on top of them.

You'll train and evaluate models for transcription quality, sentiment, and call-outcome prediction, and you'll design the experiments that prove a new model is actually better before it reaches customers. You'll work with a small, senior team where everyone ships.

Requirements:
- 5+ years in data science or applied ML, with real deep learning depth
- Strong Python; fluent with NumPy, pandas, and scikit-learn
- Production experience with PyTorch or TensorFlow
- Strong statistics and rigorous approach to A/B testing and offline evaluation
- Comfortable in Jupyter and skilled at data visualization with Matplotlib

Bonus:
- AWS SageMaker for large-scale training and deployment
- Spark for processing audio-derived features
- SQL and thoughtful feature engineering on transcript data
- Experience with speech, NLP, or audio problems

This is a fully remote role within the US, with a week-long team offsite twice a year. Compensation is $165,000-$200,000 plus equity and a generous compute budget for experimentation. We're looking for someone who cares about model quality in the wild, not just on a benchmark, and who enjoys the messy gap between a good validation score and a genuinely useful product.

postings.title: DevOps Engineer
postings.description:
Northwind Analytics is a data intelligence startup helping mid-market retailers forecast demand. We're hiring a DevOps Engineer to own the reliability and delivery pipeline behind our SaaS platform. You'll work alongside a small backend team, so you'll have real ownership over how we ship and run software.

Day to day, you'll manage our AWS infrastructure, harden our CI/CD pipelines, and improve how we deploy dozens of microservices to Kubernetes. We're moving more of our infrastructure into Terraform and want someone who genuinely enjoys turning click-ops into reproducible code. You'll also help our on-call rotation get quieter by improving alerting and dashboards.

Requirements:
- 3+ years in a DevOps, SRE, or infrastructure role
- Solid hands-on experience with AWS (EC2, VPC, IAM, RDS, S3)
- Production experience with Docker and Kubernetes
- Terraform for infrastructure as code
- Comfortable scripting in Bash and Python
- Strong Linux fundamentals and basic networking (DNS, load balancing, TLS)
- Experience building CI/CD with GitHub Actions or Jenkins

Nice to have:
- Prometheus and Grafana for observability
- Exposure to Ansible for configuration management
- Familiarity with Go
- AWS certifications (Solutions Architect or SysOps)

We offer competitive pay, equity, health coverage, and a hybrid schedule with two in-office days per week in our downtown Austin office. We care more about how you reason through incidents than how many buzzwords you know. If you like small teams and a broad mandate, we'd love to talk.

postings.title: Senior Cloud Engineer
postings.description:
Meridian Health Systems builds the scheduling and billing backbone used by regional clinics across the US. We handle sensitive data at scale, so reliability and security aren't optional. We're looking for a Senior Cloud Engineer to lead our multi-account AWS strategy and mentor a growing platform team.

You'll set the standard for how we provision, secure, and observe infrastructure. Expect to spend your time architecting network segmentation, driving our Terraform modules toward reusability, and championing least-privilege IAM across the org. You'll also partner with security and compliance teams as we maintain HIPAA and SOC 2 posture.

Requirements:
- 6+ years in cloud infrastructure or platform engineering
- Deep AWS expertise across networking, IAM, and multi-account setups
- Expert-level Terraform and infrastructure-as-code practices
- Kubernetes in production, including cluster upgrades and RBAC
- Strong grasp of cloud security controls and secrets management
- Fluent in Python and Bash for automation
- Experience with CI/CD tooling (GitLab CI or GitHub Actions)

Nice to have:
- Prometheus, Grafana, and broader observability practices
- Ansible for host configuration
- Go for building internal tooling
- Prior work in a regulated (healthcare or fintech) environment

This role is fully remote within the US with occasional travel to our Minneapolis headquarters for quarterly planning. We offer strong compensation, generous PTO, a home-office stipend, and real investment in your growth. If you want to shape a platform that clinicians depend on every day, apply and tell us about a hard infrastructure problem you solved.

postings.title: Junior DevOps Engineer
postings.description:
Brightloom Software builds workflow tools for logistics companies. We're growing our platform team and want to bring on a Junior DevOps Engineer who's early in their career but hungry to learn how modern infrastructure actually runs in production. You'll be paired with a senior mentor and get exposure to the full delivery lifecycle from commit to deploy.

You'll help maintain our CI/CD pipelines, write small automation scripts, and assist with containerizing services. Nobody expects you to know everything on day one. We care that you're curious, careful, and comfortable asking questions.

Requirements:
- Some hands-on experience with Linux (personal projects, internships, or coursework count)
- Familiarity with at least one cloud provider, ideally AWS or GCP
- Basic scripting ability in Bash or Python
- Understanding of Docker and why containers matter
- Exposure to Git and a CI system like GitHub Actions or Jenkins
- Willingness to learn networking and security fundamentals

Nice to have:
- Any exposure to Kubernetes, even in a local kind or minikube setup
- Curiosity about Terraform and infrastructure as code
- A homelab, side project, or open-source contribution to point to
- Familiarity with Prometheus or Grafana

This is a hybrid role based in Denver, with three days a week in our LoDo office. We provide mentorship, a clear growth path, health benefits, and a learning budget for certifications and courses. If you've been teaching yourself infrastructure and want a team that will invest in you, we'd love to hear from you.

postings.title: Platform / DevOps Engineer
postings.description:
Talvane Robotics develops fleet-management software for autonomous warehouse robots. Our systems run at the edge and in the cloud, which makes infrastructure genuinely interesting here. We're hiring a Platform / DevOps Engineer to strengthen the tooling our engineers rely on to build, test, and deploy.

You'll own significant parts of our GCP footprint, evolve our GitLab CI pipelines, and help standardize deployments onto Kubernetes. A big part of the job is developer experience: making the golden path fast and obvious so product teams ship confidently. You'll also improve our observability stack so we catch regressions before customers do.

Requirements:
- 4+ years in DevOps, platform, or infrastructure engineering
- Strong experience with GCP (GKE, Cloud Storage, IAM, networking)
- Production Kubernetes and Docker experience
- Terraform for provisioning infrastructure as code
- Proficient in Python and Bash
- Hands-on with GitLab CI or a comparable CI/CD platform
- Comfortable with Linux internals and container networking

Nice to have:
- Prometheus and Grafana for metrics and dashboards
- Go for writing operators or CLI tools
- Ansible experience
- Interest in edge computing or IoT deployments

This role is fully remote within European time zones, with an optional co-working budget. We work asynchronously, document heavily, and keep meetings light. Compensation is competitive for the region, and we offer 30 days of holiday plus a hardware budget. If robots and clean infrastructure both excite you, send us your application and a note about a pipeline you're proud of.

postings.title: Senior DevOps Engineer
postings.description:
Cobalt Ledger is a fintech company building payment reconciliation infrastructure for enterprises. Latency, correctness, and security matter enormously in our world. We're seeking a Senior DevOps Engineer to take ownership of our Azure environment and lead the automation of everything we can automate.

You'll architect resilient, multi-region deployments, drive our infrastructure-as-code adoption with Terraform, and tighten our security posture across the board. Expect to lead incident response, define SLOs, and mentor mid-level engineers. You'll work closely with backend teams running services on Kubernetes and help them ship faster without sacrificing safety.

Requirements:
- 6+ years in DevOps, SRE, or cloud infrastructure
- Deep Azure experience (AKS, VNets, Azure AD, Key Vault)
- Expert Terraform and IaC discipline
- Strong Kubernetes and Docker in production
- Advanced Linux, networking, and security knowledge
- Automation fluency in Python and Bash
- Experience with CI/CD via Jenkins or GitHub Actions

Nice to have:
- Prometheus, Grafana, and mature observability practices
- Go for platform tooling
- Ansible for configuration management
- Background in payments, banking, or another regulated domain

This is a hybrid position with three days per week in our Manhattan office. We offer top-of-market compensation, meaningful equity, comprehensive benefits, and a team that takes engineering craft seriously. If you've run production systems where mistakes cost real money and you still love the work, we want to meet you. Tell us about the worst outage you've owned and what you changed afterward.

postings.title: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer
postings.description:
Verdant Grid Energy builds monitoring software for renewable energy operators. Our platform ingests telemetry from thousands of solar and wind sites in real time. We're hiring a Cloud Infrastructure Engineer to help us scale ingestion, storage, and analytics reliably.

You'll work across AWS, tune our Kubernetes workloads, and expand our infrastructure-as-code coverage with Terraform. Observability is central to this role: we live in Prometheus and Grafana, and we want someone who treats good dashboards and actionable alerts as a craft. You'll also help us keep costs sane as data volume grows.

Requirements:
- 3-5 years in cloud or infrastructure engineering
- Solid AWS experience (EKS, S3, VPC, IAM)
- Production Kubernetes and Docker
- Terraform for infrastructure as code
- Strong Prometheus and Grafana skills
- Scripting in Python and Bash
- Comfortable with Linux and networking fundamentals

Nice to have:
- Experience with time-series databases at scale
- GitHub Actions or Jenkins for CI/CD
- Go for building exporters or tooling
- Ansible for config management
- Interest in the energy or sustainability sector

This role is hybrid in Seattle, two days per week in our SoDo office. We offer competitive pay, equity, strong benefits, and the satisfaction of building infrastructure that supports the energy transition. If you like big data pipelines and care about where your work points, apply with a short note on an observability problem you enjoyed solving.

postings.title: DevOps / Site Reliability Engineer
postings.description:
Halcyon Commerce powers checkout and inventory for independent online retailers. Black Friday is our Super Bowl, and reliability is the product. We're looking for a DevOps / Site Reliability Engineer who thrives on making systems boringly dependable under load.

You'll own reliability initiatives across our AWS and GCP footprint, improve our CI/CD flow, and drive capacity planning ahead of peak seasons. We run everything on Kubernetes, and you'll help us tune autoscaling, refine our observability, and reduce toil through automation. On-call is shared and humane, with a strong blameless-postmortem culture.

Requirements:
- 4+ years in SRE, DevOps, or production infrastructure
- Strong AWS experience; working knowledge of GCP a plus
- Production Kubernetes and Docker
- Terraform for infrastructure as code
- Prometheus and Grafana for observability
- Automation in Python and Bash
- CI/CD experience with GitHub Actions or GitLab CI
- Solid Linux and networking fundamentals

Nice to have:
- Load testing and capacity planning experience
- Go for tooling
- Ansible for configuration management
- Familiarity with security best practices and secrets management
- E-commerce or high-traffic consumer platform background

This is a fully remote US role with a shared, well-compensated on-call rotation. We offer competitive salary, equity, home-office stipend, and genuine respect for work-life boundaries outside peak season. If you get satisfaction from a quiet pager and a clean dashboard, we'd love to read your application. Tell us how you'd prepare a system for a 10x traffic spike.

postings.title: Cloud Engineer
postings.description:
Ashfield Digital is a consultancy that helps enterprises modernize legacy systems and move to the cloud. Every engagement is different, which keeps the work fresh and the learning constant. We're hiring a Cloud Engineer to join our delivery team and work directly with clients across finance, retail, and public sector.

You'll design and build cloud environments, write infrastructure as code, and set up CI/CD pipelines that clients can maintain after we leave. Because we serve varied clients, breadth matters. One month you might be deep in Azure, the next in AWS. You'll containerize applications, stand up Kubernetes clusters, and coach client teams on good practices.

Requirements:
- 3+ years in cloud engineering or consulting
- Hands-on experience with AWS and/or Azure
- Terraform for infrastructure as code
- Docker and Kubernetes in production
- CI/CD pipeline experience (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, or GitLab CI)
- Scripting in Python and Bash
- Comfortable with Linux, networking, and cloud security basics
- Strong communication skills for client-facing work

Nice to have:
- Ansible for configuration management
- Prometheus and Grafana for observability
- GCP experience to round out multi-cloud coverage
- Go for automation tooling
- Relevant cloud certifications (AWS, Azure, or CKA)

This role is hybrid in Toronto with occasional client-site visits. We offer competitive compensation, a certification and training budget, and exposure to a wide range of industries and stacks. If you like variety, enjoy teaching, and can context-switch without losing rigor, apply with a note about a modernization project you're proud of.

postings.title: Staff DevOps Engineer
postings.description:
Nimbus Freight Technologies builds routing and visibility software for the trucking industry. As we scale, our infrastructure needs a technical leader who can set direction without losing hands-on credibility. We're hiring a Staff DevOps Engineer to define our platform strategy and level up how the whole engineering org ships.

You'll own the long-term vision for our GCP-based platform, drive standards for infrastructure as code, and lead cross-team initiatives around reliability, security, and developer experience. This is a high-leverage role: you'll write influential Terraform modules, shape our Kubernetes platform, and mentor senior engineers while still getting your hands dirty in incidents.

Requirements:
- 8+ years in infrastructure, DevOps, or platform engineering
- Deep GCP expertise (GKE, networking, IAM, Cloud SQL)
- Mastery of Terraform and IaC at organizational scale
- Expert Kubernetes and Docker
- Strong security and networking fundamentals
- Fluent in Python and Go
- Experience defining CI/CD standards across many teams

Nice to have:
- Prometheus, Grafana, and observability platform ownership
- Ansible for configuration management
- Multi-cloud experience (AWS or Azure)
- Prior staff or principal-level technical leadership
- Logistics or supply-chain domain background

This is a fully remote US role with quarterly onsites in Chicago. We offer top-tier compensation, significant equity, and the autonomy to shape a platform used across a growing engineering organization. If you can hold a multi-year technical vision and still debug a gnarly production issue at 2am, apply and tell us about a platform bet you made that paid off.

postings.title: Data Analyst
postings.description:
Brightwave Commerce is a fast-growing DTC retail brand looking for a Data Analyst to join our small but mighty analytics team. You'll be the connective tissue between marketing, merchandising, and finance, turning messy transactional data into decisions people actually act on. A typical week might include building a weekly performance dashboard, digging into why conversion dipped in a particular region, and partnering with our growth team to design and read out an A/B test.

We care less about pedigree and more about curiosity. If you enjoy the moment a chart finally makes a stakeholder say "oh, THAT'S what's happening," you'll fit right in.

Requirements:
- 3+ years in an analytics or reporting role
- Strong SQL (joins, window functions, CTEs) against a real warehouse
- Comfort building dashboards in Tableau or Power BI
- Solid Excel skills for quick modeling and ad hoc pulls
- Experience running or analyzing A/B tests, and explaining the results in plain English
- A genuine knack for storytelling with data

Nice to have:
- Python and pandas for deeper analysis
- Familiarity with Google Analytics for web funnel work
- Exposure to Snowflake
- Basic statistics background (confidence intervals, significance)

We offer hybrid work (three days in our downtown Austin office), full health coverage, and a team that genuinely enjoys arguing about the right way to define "active user." Salary range is $95,000-$115,000 depending on experience. We review applications on a rolling basis, so don't wait to apply.

postings.title: Junior Data Analyst
postings.description:
Meridian Health Partners helps regional clinics understand their patient populations and operational bottlenecks. We're hiring a Junior Data Analyst to grow with our data team. This is an entry-level friendly role: we'll pair you with a senior analyst mentor and give you real, meaningful work from week one, not busywork.

Day to day, you'll write queries to answer questions from clinic operations leads, maintain a handful of recurring reports, and help clean and validate incoming data. Over time you'll own your own dashboards and start presenting findings directly to stakeholders.

Requirements:
- 0-2 years of experience, or a strong portfolio of school/personal projects
- Working knowledge of SQL and a willingness to level up fast
- Comfort in Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, basic formulas)
- Clear written communication; you can explain a number without a wall of jargon
- Curiosity and a habit of double-checking your own work

Nice to have:
- Any exposure to Python or pandas
- A class or two in statistics
- Familiarity with a BI tool like Tableau, Power BI, or Looker
- Interest in healthcare data (HIPAA awareness a plus)

Because we handle sensitive data, attention to detail and discretion matter enormously here. We're fully remote within the US, with an optional quarterly meetup. Compensation is $62,000-$74,000 plus benefits and a learning stipend. If you're early in your career and hungry to become a great analyst, we'd love to hear from you.

postings.title: Senior Data Analyst, Growth
postings.description:
Loft & Ledger is a fintech platform for small landlords, and our Growth team lives and dies by rigorous measurement. We're looking for a Senior Data Analyst to own experimentation and funnel analytics end to end. You won't just report numbers; you'll shape what we test, how we measure it, and which bets are worth making.

You'll design A/B tests with product managers, define the metrics that matter, and build the models that tell us whether a change actually moved the needle. You'll also mentor two junior analysts and help set standards for how we do analysis.

Requirements:
- 5+ years in analytics, with real ownership of experimentation
- Expert SQL, ideally against BigQuery
- Strong Python and pandas for statistical analysis
- Deep, practical understanding of statistics and A/B testing (power, significance, common pitfalls)
- Experience with data modeling and building clean, reusable datasets
- Exceptional storytelling with data for executive audiences

Nice to have:
- dbt for transformation and modeling
- Looker for self-serve dashboards
- Google Analytics for acquisition analysis
- Prior experience in fintech or a regulated environment

This role is hybrid in our Manhattan office (two days a week). We offer competitive equity, strong benefits, and a culture that treats analysts as decision partners, not a reporting factory. Base salary range is $150,000-$180,000. Come help us make smarter bets with sharper data.

postings.title: Data Analyst
postings.description:
Northwind Logistics moves freight across North America, and our operations generate an enormous amount of data that mostly goes unexamined. We want a Data Analyst who can change that. You'll work closely with dispatch, warehouse, and finance teams to surface inefficiencies, forecast demand, and build reporting that leadership checks every morning.

This role suits someone who likes operational problems: real constraints, physical goods, and answers that translate into fewer empty miles and faster deliveries.

Requirements:
- 2-4 years of analytics experience, ideally in ops, supply chain, or logistics
- Strong SQL for pulling and shaping data
- Advanced Excel for modeling and scenario analysis
- Experience building dashboards in Power BI
- Comfort with ETL concepts and cleaning imperfect real-world data
- Ability to translate operational questions into analysis and back into recommendations

Nice to have:
- Python and pandas for automation and heavier analysis
- Familiarity with data modeling best practices
- Exposure to Snowflake or a similar cloud warehouse
- Basic statistics for forecasting

We're an in-office team based near the Loop, four days a week, because a lot of our best insights come from walking over to the dispatch floor and asking questions. Compensation is $88,000-$105,000 with a performance bonus, plus solid benefits and a 401(k) match. If you want your analysis to have obvious, tangible impact, this is a great place to do it.

postings.title: Marketing Data Analyst
postings.description:
Cinder & Co. is a subscription coffee company, and marketing is our engine. We're hiring a Marketing Data Analyst to make sense of where our customers come from, what keeps them subscribed, and where we're wasting spend. You'll be embedded with the marketing team, acting as their in-house analyst and honest second opinion.

Expect a mix of channel performance analysis, retention and churn deep-dives, and lots of dashboard building. You'll also help design campaign experiments and read out the results without spin.

Requirements:
- 3+ years in marketing analytics or a similar analytical role
- Strong SQL against a data warehouse
- Hands-on experience with Google Analytics and attribution basics
- Dashboarding in Looker or Tableau
- Comfort with A/B testing and interpreting lift honestly
- Strong data visualization instincts and storytelling with data

Nice to have:
- Python and pandas for cohort and retention analysis
- BigQuery experience
- Familiarity with dbt or the underlying data modeling
- A working sense of statistics (you know when a result is just noise)

We're remote-first across the US and Canada, with a genuinely calm async culture and no meeting Wednesdays. Benefits include full health coverage, a home office stipend, and, obviously, excellent coffee shipped to your door. Salary range is $92,000-$110,000 USD. If you like marketing but insist on the data actually backing up the story, we should talk.

postings.title: Data Analyst
postings.description:
Halden Analytics is a boutique consultancy that helps mid-sized clients across retail and hospitality get their data house in order. As a Data Analyst here, you'll work across several client engagements at once, which means variety, fast context-switching, and a lot of learning. One month you might be untangling a retailer's sales reporting; the next, building a demand model for a hotel group.

We're a small team, so you'll have direct client contact and real ownership from the start.

Requirements:
- 3+ years of hands-on analytics experience
- Confident SQL across different database flavours
- Strong Excel for client-facing models and quick turnarounds
- Experience building clear dashboards in Power BI or Tableau
- Understanding of ETL and how to wrangle inconsistent client data
- Excellent communication; you can present to a non-technical client and hold the room

Nice to have:
- Python and pandas for repeatable analysis
- dbt and data modeling experience
- Familiarity with Snowflake or BigQuery
- A grounding in statistics
