This is a hybrid role based in central London, typically two to three days in our office near Farringdon. We offer 28 days holiday plus bank holidays, a training budget, and the chance to see how many different businesses actually run under the hood. Salary is 45,000-58,000 GBP depending on experience. If you enjoy consulting energy and want your problem set to keep changing, come join us.

postings.title: Senior Data Analyst
postings.description:
Volta Grid Energy builds software for renewable energy operators, and our data tells the story of how clean power actually flows. We're hiring a Senior Data Analyst to lead analytics for our operations and product teams. You'll own the metrics that matter, build the semantic layer others rely on, and set the bar for analytical rigor across the company.

This is a hands-on senior role, not a management track. You'll still be deep in the data, but you'll also be the person other teams come to when a number doesn't add up.

Requirements:
- 6+ years in analytics with clear examples of high-impact work
- Advanced SQL and strong data modeling skills
- Production experience with dbt for transformations
- Python and pandas for analysis beyond what SQL can do
- Comfort building and maintaining ETL pipelines
- Strong statistics foundation and a track record of clear storytelling with data

Nice to have:
- Snowflake or BigQuery at scale
- Tableau or Looker for stakeholder-facing dashboards
- Experience designing A/B tests for product features
- Interest in energy, sustainability, or grid data

We're hybrid in Denver (two days a week) with a strong preference for candidates in the Mountain time zone. Compensation is $145,000-$172,000 plus meaningful equity, and we take work-life balance seriously, including real PTO that people actually use. Help us make the energy transition legible, one clean dataset at a time.

postings.title: Product Data Analyst
postings.description:
Kestrel Learning builds an app that helps kids learn to read, and we make product decisions with evidence, not opinions. We're looking for a Product Data Analyst to partner with our product managers and designers, helping them understand how learners actually move through our lessons and where they get stuck.

You'll define product metrics, build funnels, and run experiments that shape the roadmap. Because we work with children's data, you'll also be a careful steward of privacy and quality.

Requirements:
- 3+ years in product or behavioral analytics
- Strong SQL, ideally against BigQuery
- Solid Python and pandas for cohort, funnel, and retention analysis
- Experience designing and analyzing A/B tests
- Good grasp of statistics and when a result is trustworthy
- Strong data visualization and storytelling with data skills

Nice to have:
- Looker for self-serve reporting
- dbt and thoughtful data modeling
- Experience with event tracking and instrumentation
- Familiarity with EdTech or working with under-18 user data

We're fully remote across European time zones, with a four-day core-hours overlap and flexibility around the rest. Benefits include generous parental leave, a learning budget, and a genuinely mission-driven team. Salary is 55,000-70,000 EUR depending on experience and location. If you want your analysis to help a seven-year-old finish their first book, this is a meaningful place to do it.

postings.title: Data Analyst
postings.description:
Parkside Financial Group is a wealth management firm modernizing how we understand our clients and our own operations. We're adding a Data Analyst to our growing data team, and this role is well suited to someone with one to three years of experience who wants to sharpen their craft in a supportive environment.

You'll build and maintain reporting for client services and operations leaders, answer ad hoc questions with data, and gradually take ownership of dashboards that leadership relies on. You'll sit alongside senior analysts who genuinely enjoy teaching.

Requirements:
- 1-3 years of analytics or reporting experience
- Working SQL for pulling and joining data
- Strong Excel skills, including pivot tables and clean, auditable spreadsheets
- Experience with a BI tool such as Tableau or Power BI
- Careful attention to accuracy; in finance, a wrong number has real consequences
- Clear communication with non-technical stakeholders

Nice to have:
- Python and pandas for automating repetitive pulls
- A course or practical grounding in statistics
- Exposure to data modeling or ETL concepts
- Interest in financial services

This is a hybrid role in our Boston office, three days a week. We offer strong benefits, tuition reimbursement, and a clear path from analyst toward senior analyst as you grow. Salary range is $72,000-$88,000 plus an annual bonus. If you're detail-obsessed and want to build a durable analytics career, we'd be glad to meet you.
